name: Intersection
summary: Provides static methods for the computation of intersections, projections, sections and similar.

methods.name: ArcArc
methods.signature: public static ArcArcIntersection ArcArc(Arc arcA, Arc arcB, out Point3d intersectionPoint1, out Point3d intersectionPoint2)
methods.summary: Intersects two arcs using exact calculations.

returns.doc: The intersection type.

methods.name: BrepBrep(Brep, Brep, Double, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepBrep(Brep brepA, Brep brepB, double tolerance, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects two Breps.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true if the operation was success, false otherwise. Check the output arrays to determine how the input intersects.

methods.name: BrepBrep(Brep, Brep, Double, Boolean, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepBrep(Brep brepA, Brep brepB, double tolerance, bool joinCurves, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects two Breps.

params.name: joinCurves
params.doc: If true, join the resulting curves where possible.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true if the operation was success, false otherwise. Check the output arrays to determine how the input intersects.

methods.name: BrepPlane(Brep, Plane, Double, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepPlane(Brep brep, Plane plane, double tolerance, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a Brep with an (infinite) plane.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: BrepPlane(Brep, Plane, Double, Boolean, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepPlane(Brep brep, Plane plane, double tolerance, bool joinCurves, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a Brep with an (infinite) plane.

params.name: joinCurves
params.doc: If true, join the resulting curves where possible.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: BrepSurface(Brep, Surface, Double, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepSurface(Brep brep, Surface surface, double tolerance, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a Brep and a Surface.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: true if the operation was success, false otherwise. Check the output arrays to determine how the input intersects.

methods.name: BrepSurface(Brep, Surface, Double, Boolean, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool BrepSurface(Brep brep, Surface surface, double tolerance, bool joinCurves, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a Brep and a Surface.

params.name: joinCurves
params.doc: If true, join the resulting curves where possible.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: true if the operation was success, false otherwise. Check the output arrays to determine how the input intersects.

methods.name: CircleCircle
methods.signature: public static CircleCircleIntersection CircleCircle(Circle circleA, Circle circleB, out Point3d intersectionPoint1, out Point3d intersectionPoint2)
methods.summary: Intersects two circles using exact calculations.

returns.doc: The intersection type.

methods.name: CurveBrep(Curve, Brep, Double, Curve[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public static bool CurveBrep(Curve curve, Brep brep, double tolerance, out Curve[] overlapCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve with a Brep. This function returns the 3D points of intersection and 3D overlap curves. If an error occurs while processing overlap curves, this function will return false, but it will still provide partial results.

params.name: overlapCurves
params.doc: The overlap curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: CurveBrep(Curve, Brep, Double, Double, Double[])
methods.signature: public static bool CurveBrep(Curve curve, Brep brep, double tolerance, double angleTolerance, out double[] t)
methods.summary: Intersect a curve with a Brep. This function returns the intersection parameters on the curve.

returns.doc: True on success, false on failure.

methods.name: CurveBrep(Curve, Brep, Double, Curve[], Point3d[], Double[])
methods.signature: public static bool CurveBrep(Curve curve, Brep brep, double tolerance, out Curve[] overlapCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints, out double[] curveParameters)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve with a Brep. This function returns the 3D points of intersection, curve parameters at the intersection locations, and 3D overlap curves. If an error occurs while processing overlap curves, this function will return false, but it will still provide partial results.

params.name: overlapCurves
params.doc: The overlap curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

params.name: curveParameters
params.doc: The intersection curve parameters will be returned here.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: CurveBrepFace
methods.signature: public static bool CurveBrepFace(Curve curve, BrepFace face, double tolerance, out Curve[] overlapCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve with a Brep face.

params.name: overlapCurves
params.doc: A overlap curves array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: A points array argument. This out reference is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: CurveCurve
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveCurve(Curve curveA, Curve curveB, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance)
methods.summary: Finds the intersections between two curves.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curves approach each other to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveCurveValidate
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveCurveValidate(Curve curveA, Curve curveB, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance, out int[] invalidIndices, out TextLog textLog)
methods.summary: Finds the intersections between two curves.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curves approach each other to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

params.name: invalidIndices
params.doc: The indices in the resulting CurveIntersections collection that are invalid.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log that contains tails about the invalid intersection events.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveLine
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveLine(Curve curve, Line line, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve and an infinite line.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curves approach each other to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurvePlane
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurvePlane(Curve curve, Plane plane, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve with an (infinite) plane.

returns.doc: A list of intersection events or null if no intersections were recorded.

methods.name: CurveSelf
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveSelf(Curve curve, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Finds the places where a curve intersects itself.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curve approaches itself to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveSurface(Curve, Surface, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveSurface(Curve curve, Surface surface, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve and a surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curve approaches the surface to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveSurface(Curve, Interval, Surface, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveSurface(Curve curve, Interval curveDomain, Surface surface, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a sub-curve and a surface.

params.name: curveDomain
params.doc: Domain of sub-curve to take into consideration for Intersections.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curve approaches the surface to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveSurfaceValidate(Curve, Surface, Double, Double, Int32[], TextLog)
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveSurfaceValidate(Curve curve, Surface surface, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance, out int[] invalidIndices, out TextLog textLog)
methods.summary: Intersects a curve and a surface.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curve approaches the surface to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

params.name: invalidIndices
params.doc: The indices in the resulting CurveIntersections collection that are invalid.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log that contains tails about the invalid intersection events.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: CurveSurfaceValidate(Curve, Interval, Surface, Double, Double, Int32[], TextLog)
methods.signature: public static CurveIntersections CurveSurfaceValidate(Curve curve, Interval curveDomain, Surface surface, double tolerance, double overlapTolerance, out int[] invalidIndices, out TextLog textLog)
methods.summary: Intersects a sub-curve and a surface.

params.name: curveDomain
params.doc: Domain of sub-curve to take into consideration for Intersections.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Intersection tolerance. If the curve approaches the surface to within tolerance, an intersection is assumed.

params.name: overlapTolerance
params.doc: The tolerance with which the curves are tested.

params.name: invalidIndices
params.doc: The indices in the resulting CurveIntersections collection that are invalid.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log that contains tails about the invalid intersection events.

returns.doc: A collection of intersection events.

methods.name: LineBox(Line, BoundingBox, Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public static bool LineBox(Line line, BoundingBox box, double tolerance, out Interval lineParameters)
methods.summary: Intersects an infinite line and an axis aligned bounding box.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: If tolerance > 0.0, then the intersection is performed against a box that has each side moved out by tolerance.

params.name: lineParameters
params.doc: The chord from line.PointAt(lineParameters.T0) to line.PointAt(lineParameters.T1) is the intersection.

returns.doc: true if the line intersects the box, false if no intersection occurs.

methods.name: LineBox(Line, Box, Double, Interval)
methods.signature: public static bool LineBox(Line line, Box box, double tolerance, out Interval lineParameters)
methods.summary: Intersects an infinite line with a box volume.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: If tolerance > 0.0, then the intersection is performed against a box that has each side moved out by tolerance.

params.name: lineParameters
params.doc: The chord from line.PointAt(lineParameters.T0) to line.PointAt(lineParameters.T1) is the intersection.

returns.doc: true if the line intersects the box, false if no intersection occurs.

methods.name: LineCircle
methods.signature: public static LineCircleIntersection LineCircle(Line line, Circle circle, out double t1, out Point3d point1, out double t2, out Point3d point2)
methods.summary: Intersects a line with a circle using exact calculations.

params.name: t1
params.doc: Parameter on line for first intersection.

params.name: point1
params.doc: Point on circle closest to first intersection.

params.name: t2
params.doc: Parameter on line for second intersection.

params.name: point2
params.doc: Point on circle closest to second intersection.

returns.doc: If Single is returned, only t1 and point1 will have valid values. If Multiple is returned, t2 and point2 will also be filled out.

methods.name: LineCylinder
methods.signature: public static LineCylinderIntersection LineCylinder(Line line, Cylinder cylinder, out Point3d intersectionPoint1, out Point3d intersectionPoint2)
methods.summary: Intersects a line with a cylinder using exact calculations.

returns.doc: If None is returned, the first point is the point on the line closest to the cylinder and the second point is the point on the cylinder closest to the line. If Single is returned, the first point is the point on the line and the second point is the same point on the cylinder.

methods.name: LineLine(Line, Line, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public static bool LineLine(Line lineA, Line lineB, out double a, out double b)
methods.summary: Find the unique closest-points pair between two infinite lines, if it exists.

params.name: a
params.doc: Parameter on lineA that is closest to lineB.

params.name: b
params.doc: Parameter on lineB that is closest to lineA. The shortest distance between the lines is the chord from lineA.PointAt(a) to lineB.PointAt(b)

returns.doc: true if points are found and false if the lines are numerically parallel. Numerically parallel means the 2x2 matrix: +AoA -AoB-AoB +BoB is numerically singular, where A = (lineA.To - lineA.From) and B = (lineB.To-lineB.From).

methods.name: LineLine(Line, Line, Double, Double, Double, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static bool LineLine(Line lineA, Line lineB, out double a, out double b, double tolerance, bool finiteSegments)
methods.summary: Intersects two lines.
methods.remarks: If the lines are exactly parallel, meaning the system of equations used to find a and b has no numerical solution, then false is returned. If the lines are nearly parallel, which is often numerically true even if you think the lines look exactly parallel, then the closest points are found and true is returned. So, if you care about weeding out "parallel" lines, then you need to do something like the following:

params.name: a
params.doc: Parameter on lineA that is closest to LineB. The shortest distance between the lines is the chord from lineA.PointAt(a) to lineB.PointAt(b)

params.name: b
params.doc: Parameter on lineB that is closest to LineA. The shortest distance between the lines is the chord from lineA.PointAt(a) to lineB.PointAt(b)

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: If tolerance > 0.0, then an intersection is reported only if the distance between the points is <= tolerance. If tolerance <= 0.0, then the closest point between the lines is reported.

params.name: finiteSegments
params.doc: If true, the input lines are treated as finite segments. If false, the input lines are treated as infinite lines.

returns.doc: true if a closest point can be calculated and the result passes the tolerance parameter test; otherwise false.

methods.name: LinePlane
methods.signature: public static bool LinePlane(Line line, Plane plane, out double lineParameter)
methods.summary: Intersects a line and a plane. This function only returns true if the intersection result is a single point (i.e. if the line is coincident with the plane then no intersection is assumed).

params.name: lineParameter
params.doc: Parameter on line where intersection occurs. If the parameter is not within the {0, 1} Interval then the finite segment does not intersect the plane.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: LineSphere
methods.signature: public static LineSphereIntersection LineSphere(Line line, Sphere sphere, out Point3d intersectionPoint1, out Point3d intersectionPoint2)
methods.summary: Intersects a line with a sphere using exact calculations.

returns.doc: If None is returned, the first point is the point on the line closest to the sphere and the second point is the point on the sphere closest to the line. If Single is returned, the first point is the point on the line and the second point is the same point on the sphere.

methods.name: MeshLine(Mesh, Line)
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] MeshLine(Mesh mesh, Line line)
methods.summary: Finds the intersections of a mesh and a line.

params.name: line
params.doc: The line to intersect with the mesh

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each face that was passed by the faceIds out reference. Empty if no items are found.

methods.name: MeshLine(Mesh, Line, Int32[])
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] MeshLine(Mesh mesh, Line line, out int[] faceIds)
methods.summary: Finds the intersections of a mesh and a line. The points are not necessarily sorted.

params.name: line
params.doc: The line to intersect with the mesh

params.name: faceIds
params.doc: The indices of the intersecting faces. This out reference is assigned during the call. Empty if nothing is found.

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each face that was passed by the faceIds out reference. Empty if no items are found.

methods.name: MeshLineSorted
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] MeshLineSorted(Mesh mesh, Line line, out int[] faceIds)
methods.summary: Finds the intersections of a mesh and a line. Points are sorted along the line.

params.name: line
params.doc: The line to intersect with the mesh

params.name: faceIds
params.doc: The indices of the intersecting faces. This out reference is assigned during the call. Empty if nothing is found.

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each face that was passed by the faceIds out reference. Empty if no items are found.

methods.name: MeshMesh
methods.signature: public static bool MeshMesh(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, out Polyline[] intersections, bool overlapsPolylines, out Polyline[] overlapsPolylinesResult, bool overlapsMesh, out Mesh overlapsMeshResult, TextLog textLog, CancellationToken cancel, IProgress<double> progress)
methods.summary: Intersects meshes. Overlaps and perforations are provided in the output list.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: The mesh input list. This cannot be null. Null entries are tolerated.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10.

params.name: overlapsPolylines
params.doc: If true, overlaps are computed and returned.

params.name: overlapsPolylinesResult
params.doc: If requested, overlaps are returned here.

params.name: overlapsMesh
params.doc: If true, an overlaps mesh is computed and returned.

params.name: overlapsMeshResult
params.doc: If requested, overlaps are returned here.

params.name: cancel
params.doc: A cancellation token to stop the computation at a given point.

params.name: progress
params.doc: A progress reporter to inform the user about progress, or null. The reported value is indicative.

returns.doc: True, if the operation succeeded, otherwise false.

methods.name: MeshMeshAccurate
methods.signature: public static Polyline[] MeshMeshAccurate(Mesh meshA, Mesh meshB, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects two meshes. Overlaps and near misses are handled. This is an old method kept for compatibility.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10.

returns.doc: An array of intersection and overlaps polylines.

methods.name: MeshMeshFast
methods.signature: public static Line[] MeshMeshFast(Mesh meshA, Mesh meshB)
methods.summary: This is an old overload kept for compatibility. Overlaps and near misses are ignored.

returns.doc: An array of intersection line segments, or null if no intersections were found.

methods.name: MeshMeshPredicate
methods.signature: public static bool MeshMeshPredicate(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, double tolerance, out int[] pairs, TextLog textLog)
methods.summary: Determines if meshes intersect or overlap.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: The mesh input list. This cannot be null. Null entries are tolerated.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: A tolerance value. If negative, the positive value will be used. WARNING! Good tolerance values are in the magnitude of 10^-7, or RhinoMath.SqrtEpsilon*10.

params.name: pairs
params.doc: An array containing pairs of meshes that intersect.

returns.doc: True, if meshes intersect or overlap, otherwise false. False is also returned on error, but textLog will start with Error: and contain the error.

methods.name: MeshPlane(Mesh, Plane)
methods.signature: public static Polyline[] MeshPlane(Mesh mesh, Plane plane)
methods.summary: Intersects a mesh with an infinite plane.

returns.doc: An array of polylines describing the intersection loops, or null if no intersections could be found.

methods.name: MeshPlane(Mesh, IEnumerable<Plane>)
methods.signature: public static Polyline[] MeshPlane(Mesh mesh, IEnumerable<Plane> planes)
methods.summary: Intersects a mesh with a collection of infinite planes.

returns.doc: An array of polylines describing the intersection loops, or null if no intersections could be found.

methods.name: MeshPlane(Mesh, MeshIntersectionCache, Plane, Double)
methods.signature: public static Polyline[] MeshPlane(Mesh mesh, MeshIntersectionCache cache, Plane plane, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a mesh with an infinite plane.

returns.doc: An array of polylines describing the intersection loops, or null if no intersections could be found.

methods.name: MeshPlane(Mesh, MeshIntersectionCache, IEnumerable<Plane>, Double)
methods.signature: public static Polyline[] MeshPlane(Mesh mesh, MeshIntersectionCache cache, IEnumerable<Plane> planes, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Intersects a mesh with a collection of infinite planes.

returns.doc: An array of polylines describing the intersection loops, or null if no intersections could be found.

methods.name: MeshPolyline
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] MeshPolyline(Mesh mesh, PolylineCurve curve, out int[] faceIds)
methods.summary: Finds the intersection of a mesh and a polyline. Starting from version 7, points are always sorted along the polyline.

params.name: faceIds
params.doc: The indices of the intersecting faces. This out reference is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each face that was passed by the faceIds out reference.

methods.name: MeshPolylineSorted
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] MeshPolylineSorted(Mesh mesh, PolylineCurve curve, out int[] faceIds)
methods.summary: Finds the intersection of a mesh and a polyline. Points are guaranteed to be sorted along the polyline.

params.name: faceIds
params.doc: The indices of the intersecting faces. This out reference is assigned during the call.

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each face that was passed by the faceIds out reference.

methods.name: MeshRay(Mesh, Ray3d)
methods.signature: public static double MeshRay(Mesh mesh, Ray3d ray)
methods.summary: Finds the first intersection of a ray with a mesh.

returns.doc: >= 0.0 parameter along ray if successful. < 0.0 if no intersection found.

methods.name: MeshRay(Mesh, Ray3d, Int32[])
methods.signature: public static double MeshRay(Mesh mesh, Ray3d ray, out int[] meshFaceIndices)
methods.summary: Finds the first intersection of a ray with a mesh.
methods.remarks: The ray may intersect more than one face in cases where the ray hits the edge between two faces or the vertex corner shared by multiple faces, or more than one face is coplanar or happens to be intersecting at the same location as the ray.

params.name: meshFaceIndices
params.doc: faces on mesh that ray intersects.

returns.doc: >= 0.0 parameter along ray if successful. < 0.0 if no intersection found.

methods.name: PlaneBoundingBox
methods.signature: public static bool PlaneBoundingBox(Plane plane, BoundingBox boundingBox, out Polyline polyline)
methods.summary: Intersects a plane and a bounding box.
methods.remarks: Intersects the four bounding box infinite lines in the direction of the max coordinate with the plane. Point and single line intersections are ignored.

returns.doc: True if successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: PlaneCircle
methods.signature: public static PlaneCircleIntersection PlaneCircle(Plane plane, Circle circle, out double firstCircleParameter, out double secondCircleParameter)
methods.summary: Intersects a plane with a circle using exact calculations.

params.name: firstCircleParameter
params.doc: First intersection parameter on circle if successful or RhinoMath.UnsetValue if not.

params.name: secondCircleParameter
params.doc: Second intersection parameter on circle if successful or RhinoMath.UnsetValue if not.

returns.doc: The type of intersection that occurred.

methods.name: PlanePlane
methods.signature: public static bool PlanePlane(Plane planeA, Plane planeB, out Line intersectionLine)
methods.summary: Intersects two planes and return the intersection line. If the planes are parallel or coincident, no intersection is assumed.

params.name: intersectionLine
params.doc: If this function returns true, the intersectionLine parameter will return the line where the planes intersect.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

methods.name: PlanePlanePlane
methods.signature: public static bool PlanePlanePlane(Plane planeA, Plane planeB, Plane planeC, out Point3d intersectionPoint)
methods.summary: Intersects three planes to find the single point they all share.

params.name: intersectionPoint
params.doc: Point where all three planes converge.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure. If at least two out of the three planes are parallel or coincident, failure is assumed.

methods.name: PlaneSphere
methods.signature: public static PlaneSphereIntersection PlaneSphere(Plane plane, Sphere sphere, out Circle intersectionCircle)
methods.summary: Intersects a plane with a sphere using exact calculations.

returns.doc: If None is returned, the intersectionCircle has a radius of zero and the center point is the point on the plane closest to the sphere.

methods.name: ProjectPointsToBreps
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] ProjectPointsToBreps(IEnumerable<Brep> breps, IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Vector3d direction, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Projects points onto breps.

returns.doc: Array of projected points, or null in case of any error or invalid input.

methods.name: ProjectPointsToBrepsEx
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] ProjectPointsToBrepsEx(IEnumerable<Brep> breps, IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Vector3d direction, double tolerance, out int[] indices)
methods.summary: Projects points onto breps.

params.name: indices
params.doc: Return points[i] is a projection of points[indices[i]]

returns.doc: Array of projected points, or null in case of any error or invalid input.

methods.name: ProjectPointsToMeshes
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] ProjectPointsToMeshes(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Vector3d direction, double tolerance)
methods.summary: Projects points onto meshes.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: the meshes to project on to.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Projection tolerances used for culling close points and for line-mesh intersection.

returns.doc: Array of projected points, or null in case of any error or invalid input.

methods.name: ProjectPointsToMeshesEx
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] ProjectPointsToMeshesEx(IEnumerable<Mesh> meshes, IEnumerable<Point3d> points, Vector3d direction, double tolerance, out int[] indices)
methods.summary: Projects points onto meshes.

params.name: meshes
params.doc: the meshes to project on to.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Projection tolerances used for culling close points and for line-mesh intersection.

params.name: indices
params.doc: Return points[i] is a projection of points[indices[i]]

returns.doc: Array of projected points, or null in case of any error or invalid input.

methods.name: RayShoot(IEnumerable<GeometryBase>, Ray3d, Int32)
methods.signature: public static RayShootEvent[] RayShoot(IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, Ray3d ray, int maxReflections)
methods.summary: Computes point intersections that occur when shooting a ray to a collection of surfaces and Breps.

params.name: geometry
params.doc: The collection of surfaces and Breps to intersect. Trims are ignored on Breps.

params.name: maxReflections
params.doc: The maximum number of reflections. This value should be any value between 1 and 1000, inclusive.

returns.doc: An array of RayShootEvent structs if successful, or an empty array on failure.

throws.when: maxReflections is strictly outside the [1-1000] range.

methods.name: RayShoot(Ray3d, IEnumerable<GeometryBase>, Int32)
methods.signature: public static Point3d[] RayShoot(Ray3d ray, IEnumerable<GeometryBase> geometry, int maxReflections)
methods.summary: Computes point intersections that occur when shooting a ray to a collection of surfaces and Breps.

params.name: geometry
params.doc: Only Surface and Brep objects are currently supported. Trims are ignored on Breps.

params.name: maxReflections
params.doc: The maximum number of reflections. This value should be any value between 1 and 1000, inclusive.

returns.doc: An array of points: one for each surface or Brep face that was hit, or an empty array on failure.

throws.when: maxReflections is strictly outside the [1-1000] range.

methods.name: SphereSphere
methods.signature: public static SphereSphereIntersection SphereSphere(Sphere sphereA, Sphere sphereB, out Circle intersectionCircle)
methods.summary: Intersects two spheres using exact calculations.

params.name: intersectionCircle
params.doc: If intersection is a point, then that point will be the center, radius 0.

returns.doc: The intersection type.

methods.name: SurfaceSurface
methods.signature: public static bool SurfaceSurface(Surface surfaceA, Surface surfaceB, double tolerance, out Curve[] intersectionCurves, out Point3d[] intersectionPoints)
methods.summary: Intersects two Surfaces.

params.name: intersectionCurves
params.doc: The intersection curves will be returned here.

params.name: intersectionPoints
params.doc: The intersection points will be returned here.

returns.doc: true on success, false on failure.

properties.name: MeshIntersectionsTolerancesCoefficient
properties.summary: Offers a requested adjustment coefficient for mesh-mesh intersections tolerances. The value can be used to multiply the document absolute tolerance.This is only a UI value; it is up to developer to honor (or not) this request, depending on application needs.
properties.remarks: Generally, document tolerances are around 0.001 for objects sized about 100 units. However, good mesh triangles for these objects are often a few orders of magnitude smaller than these values. This coefficient is provided to scale absolute document tolerances to values more suitable for good mesh intersections.
